text: # ISTANZE TOMCAT [nttps://toncat. apache. org/ toncat-9.0-doc/ introduction. htnl#CATALINA_HOME_and_CATALINA_BASE] (https://toncat.apache.org/ toncat-9.0-doc/ introduction. htmL#CATALINA_HOME_and_CATALINA_BASE) [nttps://toncat. apache. org/ toncat-9.0-doc/RUNNING. txt] (https://toncat . apache. org/ toncat-9. 0-doc/RUNNING. txt) [nttps://people. apache. org/~schultz/presentations/ApacheCon NA 2020/Splitting Your Tomcat Installation.pdf] (https:// people. apache. org/~schultz/presentat ions/ApacheCont20NA202020/Spl it ting'20Yourk20Toncats20lns tal Lation. pdf) - #*CATALINA_HOMEx+: Represents the root of your Tomcat installation, for example */hone/toncat/apache- toncat-9.0.10" or "C:\Progran Files\apache-toncat-9.0.10". ~ 4*CATALINA BASEx+: Represents the root of a runtime configuration of a specific Tomcat instance. If you want to have multiple Tomcat instances on one machine, use the 'CATALINA_BASE' property. The CATALINA BASE location contains configuration files, log files, deployed applications, and other runtime requirenents. CATALINA_BASE: /var/lib/toncat/geoservers/ UTILITY makebase.sh [nttps://wa. rasia. i0/blog/how-to-easily-setup-lucee-in-toncat . htnl] (https://ww. rasia. i0/blog/how-to-easily-setup- Tucee-in-toncat.htnl)
